revision.text: #redirect[[en:Shioumi_Atane]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 塩海足尼 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 塩海宿禰 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = [[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]か | 官位 = | 主君 = [[景行天皇]]、[[倭建命]] | 氏族 = [[甲斐氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[臣知津彦命]] | 兄弟 = [[御苗代命]] | 妻 = | 子 = [[速彦宿禰]] | 特記事項 = }} '''塩海足尼'''（しおみのすくね、しぶみのすくね、しおのみのすくね<ref>「[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html 酒折宮 公式サイト]」</ref>）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]で初代'''[[甲斐国造]]'''。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』や『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」ではその始祖を[[狭穂彦王]]としており、[[日下部氏|日下部連]]とは同族とされる。
[[酒折宮]]の伝承では甲斐国を訪れた[[日本武尊]]が酒折宮を発つとき、 「吾行末ここに御霊を留め鎮まり坐すべし」と言って、火打嚢を塩海足尼に授けたとされる。勅を奉戴した塩海足尼がこの火打嚢を御神体として、月見山の中腹に社殿を建てたことが神社の始まりとされる。同神社の由緒や[[磯貝正義]]・[[原秀三郎]]は酒折宮伝承に登場する東国造に任じられた'''御火焼之老人'''を塩海足尼に比定する説を唱えているが<ref>原秀三郎「王権と東方への道」『新版古代の日本⑦ 中部』[[角川書店]]、1993年。</ref>、[[鈴木真年]]はこの老人を[[筑波国造]]の祖である[[建許呂命]]と唱えており<ref>鈴木真年『日本事物原始』</ref>、[[宝賀寿男]]も国造の設置時期から考えて日本武尊の東征時に塩海足尼はまだ老人になっていなかったとしている<ref>宝賀寿男「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm 甲斐国造の系譜と一族]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。
[[美和神社 (笛吹市)|美和神社]]は[[景行天皇|景行朝]]に[[大和国]]の[[大神神社]]から[[大物主|大三輪明神]]を勧請し、塩海足尼を祭主として祀っことが起源とされる。
[[金櫻神社]]は景行天皇四十年に日本武尊が東征の帰路に参詣し、塩海宿禰に社殿の造営を命じたことが起源とされる。
なお塩海足尼の奥津城は年代的にも[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]と見られ<ref>原秀三郎「王権と東方への道」『新版古代の日本⑦ 中部』[[角川書店]]、1993年。</ref>、それに先行する[[大丸山古墳]]の被葬者は塩海足尼の父である臣知津彦命が想定される<ref>宝賀寿男「甲斐国造の系譜と一族」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。
== 系譜 == 父は[[狭穂彦王]]の孫である臣知津彦命で、子は崇玉緒神の別名を持つ速彦宿禰<ref>中田憲信「甲斐国造」『諸系譜』第七冊。</ref>。兄弟には石田公の祖・御苗代命がいる<ref>宝賀寿男「第2章 皇族系氏族 第7節 日下部氏族」『古代氏族系譜集成』上巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}

[[美和神社 (笛吹市)|美和神社]]は[[景行天皇|景行朝]]に[[大和国]]の[[大神神社]]から[[大物主|大三輪明神]]を勧請し、塩海足尼を祭主として祀っことが起源とされる。
[[金櫻神社]]は景行天皇四十年に日本武尊が東征の帰路に参詣し、塩海宿禰に社殿の造営を命じたことが起源とされる。
なお塩海足尼の奥津城は年代的にも[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]と見られ<ref>原秀三郎「王権と東方への道」『新版古代の日本⑦ 中部』[[角川書店]]、1993年。</ref>、それに先行する[[大丸山古墳]]の被葬者は塩海足尼の父である臣知津彦命が想定される<ref>宝賀寿男「甲斐国造の系譜と一族」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。

[[下部温泉]]の開湯伝説として、塩海足尼が領内巡視の折にたまたま発見し、「塩部（下部）の湯」と名付けたとするものがある<ref> 「[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ 下部]」日本温泉協会。</ref>。

国立神社では国造塩海足尼として祀られている。
== 系譜 == 父は[[狭穂彦王]]の孫である臣知津彦命で、子は崇玉緒神の別名を持つ速彦宿禰<ref>中田憲信「甲斐国造」『諸系譜』第七冊。</ref>。兄弟には石田公の祖・御苗代命がいる<ref>宝賀寿男「第2章 皇族系氏族 第7節 日下部氏族」『古代氏族系譜集成』上巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年。</ref>。

== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』や『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」ではその始祖を[[狭穂彦王]]としており、[[日下部氏|日下部連]]とは同族とされる。
[[酒折宮]]の伝承では甲斐国を訪れた[[日本武尊]]が酒折宮を発つとき、 「吾行末ここに御霊を留め鎮まり坐すべし」と言って、火打嚢を塩海足尼に授けたとされる。勅を奉戴した塩海足尼がこの火打嚢を御神体として、月見山の中腹に社殿を建てたことが神社の始まりとされる。同神社の由緒や[[磯貝正義]]・[[原秀三郎]]は酒折宮伝承に登場する東国造に任じられた'''御火焼之老人'''を塩海足尼に比定する説を唱えているが<ref>原秀三郎「王権と東方への道」『新版古代の日本⑦ 中部』[[角川書店]]、1993年。</ref>、[[鈴木真年]]はこの老人を[[筑波国造]]の祖である[[建許呂命]]と唱えており<ref>鈴木真年『日本事物原始』。</ref>、[[宝賀寿男]]も国造の設置時期から考えて日本武尊の東征時に塩海足尼はまだ老人になっていなかったとしている<ref>宝賀寿男「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm 甲斐国造の系譜と一族]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。
塩海足尼は甲斐国内の歴史的神社の創建伝承において数多く登場する。例えば[[美和神社 (笛吹市)|美和神社]]は[[景行天皇|景行朝]]に[[大和国]]の[[大神神社]]から[[大物主|大三輪明神]]を勧請し、塩海足尼を祭主として祀っことが起源とされる。[[金櫻神社]]は景行天皇四十年に日本武尊が東征の帰路に参詣し、塩海宿禰に社殿の造営を命じたことが起源とされる。[[神部神社 (山梨市)|神部神社]]は景行朝に塩海足尼が[[近江国]][[比叡]]から勧請したことが起源とされる。中牧神社は景行天皇四十年、日本武尊が東征の際に酒折宮において塩海足尼に勅し、山梨加美郷の中央茅野々丘に創祀させたことが始まりとされる。
また神社の創建伝承に限らず[[下部温泉]]の開湯伝説にも登場し、この伝説では塩海足尼が領内巡視の折にたまたま温泉を発見し、「塩部（下部）の湯」と名付けたことが下部温泉の起源とすされている<ref> 「[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ 下部]」日本温泉協会。</ref>。
なお塩海足尼の奥津城は年代的にも[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]と見られ<ref>原秀三郎「王権と東方への道」『新版古代の日本⑦ 中部』[[角川書店]]、1993年。</ref>、それに先行する[[大丸山古墳]]の被葬者は塩海足尼の父である臣知津彦命が想定される<ref>宝賀寿男「甲斐国造の系譜と一族」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。
国立神社では国造塩海足尼が祭神として祀られている。
== 系譜 == 父は[[狭穂彦王]]の孫である臣知津彦命で、子は[[玉諸神社|玉緒神]]を祭祀した速彦宿禰<ref>中田憲信「甲斐国造」『諸系譜』第七冊。</ref>。兄弟には石田公の祖・御苗代命がいる<ref>宝賀寿男「第2章 皇族系氏族 第7節 日下部氏族」『古代氏族系譜集成』上巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年。</ref>。

[[Category:甲斐氏]] {{DEFAULTSORT:しおみのすくね}} [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 塩海足尼 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 塩海宿禰、塩見宿禰、塩見足尼、塩見足尼命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = [[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]か | 官位 = 初代[[甲斐国造]] | 主君 = [[景行天皇]]、[[倭建命]] | 氏族 = [[甲斐氏|甲斐君]]祖 | 父母 = 父：臣知津彦命また意美津奴彦命 | 兄弟 = 御苗代命 | 妻 = | 子 = 速比古足尼命 | 特記事項 = }} '''塩海足尼'''（しおみのすくね、しぶみのすくね、しおのみのすくね<ref>「[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html 酒折宮 公式サイト]」</ref>）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]で初代'''[[甲斐国造]]'''。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』や『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」ではその始祖を[[狭穂彦王]]としており、[[日下部氏|日下部連]]とは同族とされる。
[[酒折宮]]の伝承では甲斐国を訪れた[[日本武尊]]が酒折宮を発つとき、 「吾行末ここに御霊を留め鎮まり坐すべし」と言って、火打嚢を塩海足尼に授けたとされる。勅を奉戴した塩海足尼がこの火打嚢を御神体として、月見山の中腹に社殿を建てたことが神社の始まりとされる。同神社の由緒や[[磯貝正義]]・[[原秀三郎]]は酒折宮伝承に登場する東国造に任じられた'''御火焼之老人'''を塩海足尼に比定する説を唱えているが<ref>原秀三郎「王権と東方への道」『新版古代の日本⑦ 中部』[[角川書店]]、1993年。</ref>、[[鈴木真年]]はこの老人を[[筑波国造]]の祖である[[建許呂命]]と唱えており<ref>鈴木真年『日本事物原始』。</ref>、[[宝賀寿男]]も国造の設置時期から考えて日本武尊の東征時に塩海足尼はまだ老人になっていなかったとしている<ref>宝賀寿男「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm 甲斐国造の系譜と一族]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。
塩海足尼は甲斐国内の歴史的神社の創建伝承において数多く登場する。例えば[[美和神社 (笛吹市)|美和神社]]は[[景行天皇|景行朝]]に[[大和国]]の[[大神神社]]から[[大物主|大三輪明神]]を勧請し、塩海足尼を祭主として祀っことが起源とされる。[[金櫻神社]]は景行天皇四十年に日本武尊が東征の帰路に参詣し、塩海宿禰に社殿の造営を命じたことが起源とされる。[[神部神社 (山梨市)|神部神社]]は景行朝に塩海足尼が[[近江国]][[比叡]]から勧請したことが起源とされる。中牧神社は景行天皇四十年、日本武尊が東征の際に酒折宮において塩海足尼に勅し、山梨加美郷の中央茅野々丘に創祀させたことが始まりとされる。
また神社の創建伝承に限らず[[下部温泉]]の開湯伝説にも登場し、この伝説では塩海足尼が領内巡視の折にたまたま温泉を発見し、「塩部（下部）の湯」と名付けたことが下部温泉の起源とされている<ref> 「[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ 下部]」日本温泉協会。</ref>。
なお塩海足尼の奥津城は年代的にも[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]と見られ<ref>原秀三郎「王権と東方への道」『新版古代の日本⑦ 中部』[[角川書店]]、1993年。</ref>、それに先行する[[大丸山古墳]]の被葬者は塩海足尼の父である臣知津彦命が想定される<ref>宝賀寿男「甲斐国造の系譜と一族」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。
国立神社では国造塩海足尼が祭神として祀られている。
== 系譜 == 父は[[狭穂彦王]]の孫である臣知津彦命で、子は[[玉諸神社|玉緒神]]を祭祀した速彦宿禰<ref>中田憲信「甲斐国造」『諸系譜』第七冊。</ref>。兄弟には石田公の祖・御苗代命がいる<ref>宝賀寿男「第2章 皇族系氏族 第7節 日下部氏族」『古代氏族系譜集成』上巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年。</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] *[[国造]]

なお塩海足尼の奥津城は年代的にも[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]と見られ<ref>原秀三郎「王権と東方への道」『新版古代の日本⑦ 中部』[[角川書店]]、1993年。</ref>、それに先行する[[大丸山古墳]]の被葬者は塩海足尼の父である臣知津彦命が想定される<ref>宝賀寿男「甲斐国造の系譜と一族」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。
国立神社では国造塩海足尼が祭神として祀られている。
== 系譜 == 父は[[狭穂彦王]]の孫である臣知津彦命で、子は[[玉諸神社|玉緒神]]を祭祀した速彦宿禰<ref>中田憲信「甲斐国造」『諸系譜』第七冊。</ref>で、兄弟には石田公の祖・御苗代命がいる<ref>宝賀寿男「第2章 皇族系氏族 第7節 日下部氏族」『古代氏族系譜集成』上巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年。</ref>とする系図が存在するが、近世のものであり、信憑性は低く学界からも認められていない。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 塩海足尼 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 塩海宿禰、塩見宿禰、塩見足尼、塩見足尼命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = [[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]か | 官位 = 初代[[甲斐国造]] | 主君 = [[景行天皇]]、[[倭建命]] | 氏族 = [[甲斐氏|甲斐君]]祖 | 父母 = 父：臣知津彦命また意美津奴彦命？ | 兄弟 = 御苗代命？ | 妻 = | 子 = 速比古足尼命？ | 特記事項 = }} '''塩海足尼'''（しおみのすくね、しぶみのすくね、しおのみのすくね<ref>「[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html 酒折宮 公式サイト]」</ref>）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]で初代'''[[甲斐国造]]'''。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』や『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」ではその始祖を[[狭穂彦王]]としており、[[日下部氏|日下部連]]とは同族とされる。
[[酒折宮]]の伝承では甲斐国を訪れた[[日本武尊]]が酒折宮を発つとき、 「吾行末ここに御霊を留め鎮まり坐すべし」と言って、火打嚢を塩海足尼に授けたとされる。勅を奉戴した塩海足尼がこの火打嚢を御神体として、月見山の中腹に社殿を建てたことが神社の始まりとされる。同神社の由緒や[[磯貝正義]]・[[原秀三郎]]は酒折宮伝承に登場する東国造に任じられた'''御火焼之老人'''を塩海足尼に比定する説を唱えているが<ref>原秀三郎「王権と東方への道」『新版古代の日本⑦ 中部』[[角川書店]]、1993年。</ref>、[[鈴木真年]]はこの老人を[[筑波国造]]の祖である[[建許呂命]]と唱えており<ref>鈴木真年『日本事物原始』。</ref>。
塩海足尼は甲斐国内の歴史的神社の創建伝承において数多く登場する。例えば[[美和神社 (笛吹市)|美和神社]]は[[景行天皇|景行朝]]に[[大和国]]の[[大神神社]]から[[大物主|大三輪明神]]を勧請し、塩海足尼を祭主として祀っことが起源とされる。[[金櫻神社]]は景行天皇四十年に日本武尊が東征の帰路に参詣し、塩海宿禰に社殿の造営を命じたことが起源とされる。[[神部神社 (山梨市)|神部神社]]は景行朝に塩海足尼が[[近江国]][[比叡]]から勧請したことが起源とされる。中牧神社は景行天皇四十年、日本武尊が東征の際に酒折宮において塩海足尼に勅し、山梨加美郷の中央茅野々丘に創祀させたことが始まりとされる。
また神社の創建伝承に限らず[[下部温泉]]の開湯伝説にも登場し、この伝説では塩海足尼が領内巡視の折にたまたま温泉を発見し、「塩部（下部）の湯」と名付けたことが下部温泉の起源とされている<ref> 「[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ 下部]」日本温泉協会。</ref>。
なお塩海足尼の奥津城は年代的に[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]と見られる<ref>原秀三郎「王権と東方への道」『新版古代の日本⑦ 中部』[[角川書店]]、1993年。</ref>。
国立神社では国造塩海足尼が祭神として祀られている。
== 系譜 == 父は[[狭穂彦王]]の孫である臣知津彦命で、子は[[玉諸神社|玉緒神]]を祭祀した速彦宿禰<ref>中田憲信「甲斐国造」『諸系譜』第七冊。</ref>で、兄弟には石田公の祖・御苗代命がいる<ref>宝賀寿男「第2章 皇族系氏族 第7節 日下部氏族」『古代氏族系譜集成』上巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年。</ref>とする系図が存在するが、近世のものであり、信憑性は低く学界からも認められていない。

revision.comment: [[Special:Contributions/おっふ|おっふ]] ([[User talk:おっふ|会話]]) による ID:89477007 の版を[[H:RV|取り消し]]ただの個人サイトではなく、20年以上も前から続く、[[宝賀寿男]]公認の古代氏族研究会公認ＨＰ（関連の日本家系図学会ですら、最近（2021年）やっと公式HPを持てたように、古代氏族研究会の公式HPはいまだなく、このHPが代理を務めている）。暇つぶしのような感覚で削除されている為、復活。
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 塩海足尼 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 塩海宿禰、塩見宿禰、塩見足尼、塩見足尼命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = [[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]か | 官位 = 初代[[甲斐国造]] | 主君 = [[景行天皇]]、[[倭建命]] | 氏族 = [[甲斐氏|甲斐君]]祖 | 父母 = 父：臣知津彦命また意美津奴彦命？ | 兄弟 = 御苗代命？ | 妻 = | 子 = 速比古足尼命？ | 特記事項 = }} '''塩海足尼'''（しおみのすくね、しぶみのすくね、しおのみのすくね<ref>「[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html 酒折宮 公式サイト]」</ref>）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]で初代'''[[甲斐国造]]'''。

[[酒折宮]]の伝承では甲斐国を訪れた[[日本武尊]]が酒折宮を発つとき、 「吾行末ここに御霊を留め鎮まり坐すべし」と言って、火打嚢を塩海足尼に授けたとされる。勅を奉戴した塩海足尼がこの火打嚢を御神体として、月見山の中腹に社殿を建てたことが神社の始まりとされる。同神社の由緒や[[磯貝正義]]・[[原秀三郎]]は酒折宮伝承に登場する東国造に任じられた'''御火焼之老人'''を塩海足尼に比定する説を唱えているが<ref>原秀三郎「王権と東方への道」『新版古代の日本⑦ 中部』[[角川書店]]、1993年。</ref>、[[鈴木真年]]はこの老人を[[筑波国造]]の祖である[[建許呂命]]と唱えており<ref>鈴木真年『日本事物原始』。</ref>、[[宝賀寿男]]も国造の設置時期から考えて日本武尊の東征時に塩海足尼はまだ老人になっていなかったとしている<ref>宝賀寿男「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm 甲斐国造の系譜と一族]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 塩海足尼 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 塩海宿禰、塩見宿禰、塩見足尼、塩見足尼命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = [[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]か | 官位 = 初代[[甲斐国造]] | 主君 = [[景行天皇]]、[[倭建命]] | 氏族 = [[甲斐氏|甲斐君]]祖 | 父母 = 父：臣知津彦命また意美津奴彦命？ | 兄弟 = 御苗代命？ | 妻 = | 子 = 速比古足尼命？ | 特記事項 = }} '''塩海足尼'''（しおみのすくね、しぶみのすくね、しおのみのすくね<ref>「[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html 酒折宮 公式サイト]」</ref>）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]で初代'''[[甲斐国造]]'''。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』や『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」ではその始祖を[[狭穂彦王]]としており、[[日下部氏|日下部連]]とは同族とされる。
[[酒折宮]]の伝承では甲斐国を訪れた[[日本武尊]]が酒折宮を発つとき、 「吾行末ここに御霊を留め鎮まり坐すべし」と言って、火打嚢を塩海足尼に授けたとされる。勅を奉戴した塩海足尼がこの火打嚢を御神体として、月見山の中腹に社殿を建てたことが神社の始まりとされる。同神社の由緒や[[磯貝正義]]・[[原秀三郎]]は酒折宮伝承に登場する東国造に任じられた'''御火焼之老人'''を塩海足尼に比定する説を唱えているが<ref>原秀三郎「王権と東方への道」『新版古代の日本⑦ 中部』[[角川書店]]、1993年。</ref>、[[鈴木真年]]はこの老人を[[筑波国造]]の祖である[[建許呂命]]と唱えており<ref>鈴木真年『日本事物原始』。</ref>、[[宝賀寿男]]も国造の設置時期から考えて日本武尊の東征時に塩海足尼はまだ老人になっていなかったとしている<ref>宝賀寿男「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm 甲斐国造の系譜と一族]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。
塩海足尼は甲斐国内の歴史的神社の創建伝承において数多く登場する。例えば[[美和神社 (笛吹市)|美和神社]]は[[景行天皇|景行朝]]に[[大和国]]の[[大神神社]]から[[大物主|大三輪明神]]を勧請し、塩海足尼を祭主として祀っことが起源とされる。[[金櫻神社]]は景行天皇四十年に日本武尊が東征の帰路に参詣し、塩海宿禰に社殿の造営を命じたことが起源とされる。[[神部神社 (山梨市)|神部神社]]は景行朝に塩海足尼が[[近江国]][[比叡]]から勧請したことが起源とされる。中牧神社は景行天皇四十年、日本武尊が東征の際に酒折宮において塩海足尼に勅し、山梨加美郷の中央茅野々丘に創祀させたことが始まりとされる。
また神社の創建伝承に限らず[[下部温泉]]の開湯伝説にも登場し、この伝説では塩海足尼が領内巡視の折にたまたま温泉を発見し、「塩部（下部）の湯」と名付けたことが下部温泉の起源とされている<ref> 「[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ 下部]」日本温泉協会。</ref>。
なお塩海足尼の奥津城は年代的にも[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]と見られ<ref>原秀三郎「王権と東方への道」『新版古代の日本⑦ 中部』[[角川書店]]、1993年。</ref>、それに先行する[[大丸山古墳]]の被葬者は塩海足尼の父である臣知津彦命が想定される<ref>宝賀寿男「甲斐国造の系譜と一族」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。
国立神社では国造塩海足尼が祭神として祀られている。
== 系譜 == 父は[[狭穂彦王]]の孫である臣知津彦命で、子は[[玉諸神社|玉緒神]]を祭祀した速彦宿禰<ref>中田憲信「甲斐国造」『諸系譜』第七冊。</ref>で、兄弟には石田公の祖・御苗代命がいる<ref>宝賀寿男「第2章 皇族系氏族 第7節 日下部氏族」『古代氏族系譜集成』上巻、古代氏族研究会、1986年。</ref>とする系図が存在するが、近世のものであり、信憑性は低く学界からも認められていない。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] *[[国造]]
[[Category:甲斐氏]] {{DEFAULTSORT:しおみのすくね}} [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:甲斐国の人物]] [[Category:山梨県の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 塩海足尼 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 塩海宿禰、塩見宿禰、塩見足尼、塩見足尼命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = 初代[[甲斐国造]] | 主君 = [[景行天皇]]、[[倭建命]] | 氏族 = [[甲斐氏|甲斐君]]祖 | 父母 = | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }} '''塩海足尼'''（しおみのすくね、しぶみのすくね、しおのみのすくね<ref>「[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html 酒折宮 公式サイト]」</ref>）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]で初代'''[[甲斐国造]]'''。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』や『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」ではその始祖を[[狭穂彦王]]としており、[[日下部氏|日下部連]]とは同族とされる。
[[酒折宮]]の伝承では甲斐国を訪れた[[日本武尊]]が酒折宮を発つとき、 「吾行末ここに御霊を留め鎮まり坐すべし」と言って、火打嚢を塩海足尼に授けたとされる。勅を奉戴した塩海足尼がこの火打嚢を御神体として、月見山の中腹に社殿を建てたことが神社の始まりとされる。同神社の由緒や[[磯貝正義]]・[[原秀三郎]]は酒折宮伝承に登場する東国造に任じられた'''御火焼之老人'''を塩海足尼に比定する説を唱えているが<ref name="#1">原秀三郎「王権と東方への道」『新版古代の日本⑦ 中部』[[角川書店]]、1993年。</ref>、[[鈴木真年]]はこの老人を[[筑波国造]]の祖である[[建許呂命]]と唱えており<ref>鈴木真年『日本事物原始』。</ref>、[[宝賀寿男]]も国造の設置時期から考えて日本武尊の東征時に塩海足尼はまだ老人になっていなかったとしている<ref>宝賀寿男「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/kaikokuso/kaikok1.htm 甲斐国造の系譜と一族]」『古樹紀之房間』、2016年。</ref>。
塩海足尼は甲斐国内の歴史的神社の創建伝承において数多く登場する。例えば[[美和神社 (笛吹市)|美和神社]]は[[景行天皇|景行朝]]に[[大和国]]の[[大神神社]]から[[大物主|大三輪明神]]を勧請し、塩海足尼を祭主として祀っことが起源とされる。[[金櫻神社]]は景行天皇四十年に日本武尊が東征の帰路に参詣し、塩海宿禰に社殿の造営を命じたことが起源とされる。[[神部神社 (山梨市)|神部神社]]は景行朝に塩海足尼が[[近江国]][[比叡]]から勧請したことが起源とされる。中牧神社は景行天皇四十年、日本武尊が東征の際に酒折宮において塩海足尼に勅し、山梨加美郷の中央茅野々丘に創祀させたことが始まりとされる。
また神社の創建伝承に限らず[[下部温泉]]の開湯伝説にも登場し、この伝説では塩海足尼が領内巡視の折にたまたま温泉を発見し、「塩部（下部）の湯」と名付けたことが下部温泉の起源とされている<ref> 「[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ 下部]」日本温泉協会。</ref>。
国立神社では国造塩海足尼が祭神として祀られている。
== 系譜 == [[狭穂彦王]]の子孫か。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}

国立神社では国造塩海足尼が祭神として祀られている。
== 系譜 == [[狭穂彦王]]の3世孫・臣知津彦公の子。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] *[[国造]]
[[Category:甲斐氏]] {{DEFAULTSORT:しおみのすくね}} [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:国造主]][[Category:甲斐国の人物]] [[Category:山梨県の歴史]]

revision.comment: 戻し：「国造主」という学術用語なし。勝手な造語につき。
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 塩海足尼 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 塩海宿禰、塩見宿禰、塩見足尼、塩見足尼命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = 初代[[甲斐国造]] | 主君 = [[景行天皇]]、[[倭建命]] | 氏族 = [[甲斐氏|甲斐君]]祖 | 父母 = 臣知津彦公 | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }} '''塩海足尼'''（しおみのすくね、しぶみのすくね、しおのみのすくね<ref>「[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html 酒折宮 公式サイト]」</ref>）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]で初代'''[[甲斐国造]]'''。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』や『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」ではその始祖を[[狭穂彦王]]としており、[[日下部氏|日下部連]]とは同族とされる。
[[酒折宮]]の伝承では甲斐国を訪れた[[日本武尊]]が酒折宮を発つとき、 「吾行末ここに御霊を留め鎮まり坐すべし」と言って、火打嚢を塩海足尼に授けたとされる。勅を奉戴した塩海足尼がこの火打嚢を御神体として、月見山の中腹に社殿を建てたことが神社の始まりとされる。同神社の由緒や[[磯貝正義]]・[[原秀三郎]]は酒折宮伝承に登場する東国造に任じられた'''御火焼之老人'''を塩海足尼に比定する説を唱えている<ref name="#1">原秀三郎「王権と東方への道」『新版古代の日本⑦ 中部』[[角川書店]]、1993年。</ref>。
塩海足尼は甲斐国内の歴史的神社の創建伝承において数多く登場する。例えば[[美和神社 (笛吹市)|美和神社]]は[[景行天皇|景行朝]]に[[大和国]]の[[大神神社]]から[[大物主|大三輪明神]]を勧請し、塩海足尼を祭主として祀っことが起源とされる。[[金櫻神社]]は景行天皇四十年に日本武尊が東征の帰路に参詣し、塩海宿禰に社殿の造営を命じたことが起源とされる。[[神部神社 (山梨市)|神部神社]]は景行朝に塩海足尼が[[近江国]][[比叡]]から勧請したことが起源とされる。中牧神社は景行天皇四十年、日本武尊が東征の際に酒折宮において塩海足尼に勅し、山梨加美郷の中央茅野々丘に創祀させたことが始まりとされる。
また神社の創建伝承に限らず[[下部温泉]]の開湯伝説にも登場し、この伝説では塩海足尼が領内巡視の折にたまたま温泉を発見し、「塩部（下部）の湯」と名付けたことが下部温泉の起源とされている<ref> 「[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ 下部]」日本温泉協会。</ref>。
国立神社では国造塩海足尼が祭神として祀られている。
== 系譜 == [[狭穂彦王]]の3世孫・臣知津彦公の子。

revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = First [[Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko (甲斐国造)]] | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorinomiya]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|en|甲斐国造}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|古事記}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|en|先代旧事本紀}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|en|狭穂彦王}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorinomiya|ja|酒折宮}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorinomiya.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}

Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorinomiya]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorinomiya|ja|酒折宮}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorinomiya.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorinomiya]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorinomiya|ja|酒折宮}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorinomiya.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}

{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorinomiya]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorinomiya|ja|酒折宮}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorinomiya.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorinomiya]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorinomiya|ja|酒折宮}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorinomiya.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorinomiya]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorinomiya|ja|酒折宮}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorinomiya.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}.

== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorinomiya|ja|酒折宮}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorinomiya.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}

{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}.

For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王}}.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}

Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}.

'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}.

revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}

"Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.

Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}.

'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>

For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.

Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}.

Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.

Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}.

{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
