revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}} * {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}

{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=draft}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=draft}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=draft}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=draft}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=draft}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=draft}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=draft}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=draft}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主|12=draft}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|ja|大神神社|12=draft}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=draft}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社|12=draft}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|12=draft}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|12=draft}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社|12=draft}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=draft}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造|12=draft}}

25 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:塩海足尼 up to rev 99874842
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 塩海足尼 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 塩海宿禰、塩見宿禰、塩見足尼、塩見足尼命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = 初代[[甲斐国造]] | 主君 = [[景行天皇]]、[[倭建命]] | 氏族 = [[甲斐氏|甲斐君]]祖 | 父母 = 臣知津彦公 | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }} '''塩海足尼'''（しおみのすくね、しぶみのすくね、しおのみのすくね<ref>「[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html 酒折宮 公式サイト]」</ref>）は、[[古墳時代]]の[[豪族]]で初代'''[[甲斐国造]]'''。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』や『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」ではその始祖を[[狭穂彦王]]としており、[[日下部氏|日下部連]]とは同族とされる。
[[酒折宮]]の伝承では甲斐国を訪れた[[日本武尊]]が酒折宮を発つとき、 「吾行末ここに御霊を留め鎮まり坐すべし」と言って、火打嚢を塩海足尼に授けたとされる。勅を奉戴した塩海足尼がこの火打嚢を御神体として、月見山の中腹に社殿を建てたことが神社の始まりとされる。同神社の由緒や[[磯貝正義]]・[[原秀三郎]]は酒折宮伝承に登場する東国造に任じられた'''御火焼之老人'''を塩海足尼に比定する説を唱えている<ref name="#1">原秀三郎「王権と東方への道」『新版古代の日本⑦ 中部』[[角川書店]]、1993年。</ref>。
塩海足尼は甲斐国内の歴史的神社の創建伝承において数多く登場する。例えば[[美和神社 (笛吹市)|美和神社]]は[[景行天皇|景行朝]]に[[大和国]]の[[大神神社]]から[[大物主|大三輪明神]]を勧請し、塩海足尼を祭主として祀っことが起源とされる。[[金櫻神社]]は景行天皇四十年に日本武尊が東征の帰路に参詣し、塩海宿禰に社殿の造営を命じたことが起源とされる。[[神部神社 (山梨市)|神部神社]]は景行朝に塩海足尼が[[近江国]][[比叡]]から勧請したことが起源とされる。中牧神社は景行天皇四十年、日本武尊が東征の際に酒折宮において塩海足尼に勅し、山梨加美郷の中央茅野々丘に創祀させたことが始まりとされる。
また神社の創建伝承に限らず[[下部温泉]]の開湯伝説にも登場し、この伝説では塩海足尼が領内巡視の折にたまたま温泉を発見し、「塩部（下部）の湯」と名付けたことが下部温泉の起源とされている<ref> 「[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ 下部]」日本温泉協会。</ref>。
国立神社では国造塩海足尼が祭神として祀られている。
== 系譜 == [[狭穂彦王]]の3世孫・臣知津彦公の子。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] *[[国造]]

国立神社では国造塩海足尼が祭神として祀られている。

Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>

"Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>

{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_Rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|qq=}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|qq=}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] from the [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|qq=}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobe Onsen|qq=}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuritsu Shrine|qq=}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|qq=}} * [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]
{{translated page|ja|塩海足尼|version=99874842|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Kai clan
revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_Rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|qq=}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|qq=}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] from the [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 静岡浅間神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobe Onsen|qq=}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuritsu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|qq=}} * [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]

{{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_Rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|qq=}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|qq=}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] from the [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 静岡浅間神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobe Onsen|qq=}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuritsu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.

{{Infobox person}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q100453532]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_Rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|qq=}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|qq=}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] from the [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 静岡浅間神社}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobe Onsen|qq=}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuritsu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.

For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] from the [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 静岡浅間神社|1=Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q500368}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobe Onsen|qq=|1=Shimobe Spa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361358}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>

{{Infobox person}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q100453532]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_Rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|qq=|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|qq=|1=Sakaori-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] from the [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 静岡浅間神社|1=Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q500368}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobe Onsen|qq=|1=Shimobe Spa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361358}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuritsu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.

Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.

{{Infobox person}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q100453532]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_Rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|qq=|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|qq=|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] from the [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 静岡浅間神社|1=Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q500368}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobe Onsen|qq=|1=Shimobe Spa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361358}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuritsu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塩海足尼]] {{Infobox person}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q100453532]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_Rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] from the [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 静岡浅間神社|1=Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q500368}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobe Onsen|1=Shimobe Spa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361358}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuritsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.

According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] from the [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 静岡浅間神社|1=Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q500368}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobe Onsen|1=Shimobe Spa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361358}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuritsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.

== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] from the [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 静岡浅間神社|1=Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q500368}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobe Onsen|1=Shimobe Spa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361358}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuritsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塩海足尼]] {{Infobox person}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q100453532]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_Rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] from the [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 静岡浅間神社|1=Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q500368}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobe Onsen|1=Shimobe Spa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361358}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuritsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塩海足尼]] {{Infobox person}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q100453532]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_Rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] from the [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 静岡浅間神社|1=Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q500368}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobe Onsen|1=Shimobe Spa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361358}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuritsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.

== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of people before the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people before the Asuka period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}} * [[:en:Kuni no miyatsuko|kuni no miyatsuko]]

'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] from the [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 静岡浅間神社|1=Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q500368}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobe Onsen|1=Shimobe Spa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361358}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuritsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.

Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobe Onsen|1=Shimobe Spa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361358}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuritsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塩海足尼]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塩海足尼]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塩海足尼]] {{Infobox person}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q100453532]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_Rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] from the [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 静岡浅間神社|1=Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q500368}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobe Onsen|1=Shimobe Spa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361358}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuritsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塩海足尼]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塩海足尼]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塩海足尼]] {{Infobox person}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q100453532]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_Rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] from the [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 静岡浅間神社|1=Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q500368}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobe Onsen|1=Shimobe Spa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361358}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuritsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.

'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] from the [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 静岡浅間神社|1=Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q500368}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobe Onsen|1=Shimobe Spa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361358}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuritsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.

Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobe Onsen|1=Shimobe Spa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361358}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuritsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塩海足尼]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塩海足尼]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塩海足尼]] {{Infobox person}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q100453532]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_Rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi]] from the [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmiwa Shrine]] in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 静岡浅間神社|1=Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q500368}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobe Onsen|1=Shimobe Spa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361358}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuritsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.
