<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塩海足尼]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塩海足尼]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塩海足尼]] {{Infobox person}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q100453532]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_Rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}} | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} and the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 静岡浅間神社|1=Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q500368}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobe Onsen|1=Shimobe Spa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361358}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuritsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.

"Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塩海足尼]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塩海足尼]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:塩海足尼]] {{Infobox person}} {{Infobox noble | name = Shiomi no Sukune (塩海足尼) | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | other_names = 塩海宿禰, 塩見宿禰, 塩見足尼, 塩見足尼命 | posthumous_name = | deity_title = | buddhist_name = | resting_place = | court_Rank = First {{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} | master = [[Emperor Keikō]], {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}} | family = Ancestor of {{ill|Kai clan|ja|甲斐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai clan|1=Kai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577901}} | parents = Father: Chitohiko no Mikoto | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} and the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 静岡浅間神社|1=Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q500368}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobe Onsen|1=Shimobe Spa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361358}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuritsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.

'''Shiomi no Sukune''' (塩海足尼, also rendered as Shibumi no Sukune or Shiomi no Sukune)<ref>[http://sakaorinomiya.jp/s/top.html Official Website of Sakaorimiya Shrine]</ref> was a prominent {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} noble and the first '''{{ill|Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|甲斐国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' (Governor of Kai Province).
== Overview ==
In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, Shiomi no Sukune is identified as a descendant of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, suggesting a connection with the {{ill|Kusakabe clan|ja|日下部氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusakabe clan|qq=}}.
According to the traditions of {{ill|Sakaorimiya Shrine|ja|酒折宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakaorimiya Shrine|1=Sakaori-no-Miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11644327}}, when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} visited Kai Province, he entrusted Shiomi no Sukune with a sacred flint pouch, saying, "May my spirit remain here and be venerated." Shiomi no Sukune established a shrine on the slopes of Tsukimi Mountain, enshrining the flint as the deity. Scholars such as Masayoshi Isogai and Hidesaburō Hara associate the "Elder of Sacred Fires" mentioned in the legends with Shiomi no Sukune.<ref name="#1">Hara, Hidesaburō. "Paths to the East and Royal Authority," in *Ancient Japan Volume 7: Chubu*, Kadokawa Shoten, 1993.</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune appears in the founding legends of numerous historic shrines in Kai Province. For example: - {{ill|Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki City)|lt=Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|ja|美和神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa Shrine (Fuefuki)|qq=}} is said to have originated when he enshrined the deity {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|1=Ōmononushi}} from the {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} in the Keikō era. - {{ill|Kanazakura Shrine|ja|金櫻神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanazakura Shrine|qq=}} traces its roots to Shiomi no Sukune's construction of a hall for Yamato Takeru's worship in the 40th year of Emperor Keikō's reign. - {{ill|Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja|神部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinbu Shrine (Yamanashi)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 静岡浅間神社|1=Shizuoka Sengen Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q500368}} attributes its founding to Shiomi no Sukune bringing a deity from Mount Hiei in {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}}. - Nakamaki Shrine was established by Shiomi no Sukune at Emperor Keikō's command after Yamato Takeru's visit to Sakaorimiya Shrine.
Beyond shrines, Shiomi no Sukune is also central to the foundation myth of {{ill|Shimobe Onsen|ja|下部温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimobe Onsen|1=Shimobe Spa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361358}}, which recounts his discovery of the hot springs during a regional inspection.<ref>Japan Spa Association, "[https://www.spa.or.jp/kokumin/971/ Shimobe Onsen]."</ref>
Shiomi no Sukune is enshrined as a deity at {{ill|Kokuritsu Shrine|ja|国立神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuritsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Genealogy ==
Shiomi no Sukune was the child of Chitohiko no Mikoto, the great-grandson of {{ill|Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Hikoo no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}.
